prevalence.trend: stable; improved recognition in rheumatology clinics

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; chronic sicca symptoms and fatigue

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Sicca symptoms with positive anti-SSA/SSB or salivary gland biopsy; ocular tests (Schirmer) and salivary flow

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Rheumatology referral for autoantibodies and biopsy when needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Artificial tears/saliva, pilocarpine when available, hydroxychloroquine for systemic symptoms; monitor lymphoma risk
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to rheumatology and specialty testing in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Core symptomatic treatments available and expanding rheumatology services
